scenario: generic-files adapter recognize(component, file) for every file in tests/fixtures/bundles/generic-files/. Arbitrary files → document / script / file. Explicit-config ONLY (never auto-probed). SPECIFICATION golden.
adapterStatus: NOT IMPLEMENTED — spec-authored target the future generic-files adapter must reproduce (future Chunk-2 format-adapter work item).
specSource: docs/design/akm-0.9.0-bundle-adapter-spec.md §7 (generic-files adapter row: 'any leftover file → document/script/file; explicit-config ONLY (never auto-selected, §1.2)'), §1.2 (generic-files is never in the auto-probe order; looksLikeRoot never fires).
realWorldSource: AKM-native fallback family; the v0.2 sensitive-content refusal was withdrawn (deviation §4.3c) — the user indexes exactly what they pointed generic-files at
notes: SPECIFICATION golden — no generic-files adapter module exists. This adapter is EXPLICIT-CONFIG ONLY: it is never in the §1.2 auto-probe order, and its looksLikeRoot must NOT fire on any root (a user opts in deliberately).

TYPE MAPPING (FLAG — spec names the three types 'document/script/file' but does NOT define the exact classification rules). Best-reasoned default recorded here and in the methodology doc: files with a SCRIPT_EXTENSIONS extension → `script` (conceptId keeps the extension, like the akm script type); markdown/plain-text files → `document`; everything else (binary, data, unknown) → `file`. The adapter author finalizes the exact predicate.

conceptId: `document` follows OKF markdown convention when applicable, but generic-files generally KEEPS the file's natural path/extension (it indexes what it was pointed at); `script` and `file` keep the extension. Recorded per-file below.

build.sh.name: build.sh
build.sh.derivation: .sh ∈ SCRIPT_EXTENSIONS → script; conceptId KEEPS the extension (mirrors the akm script type).

data.csv.name: data.csv
data.csv.derivation: not markdown, not a script extension → file (the catch-all); conceptId keeps the natural path/extension.
